seed_problem: Partition a weighted graph into two balanced halves maximizing cut weight; checker validates balance and scores cut/baseline.
mutation: input+output: instantiate balanced max-cut as a fresh non-bipartite instance (heavy grid backbone + lighter random chords creating odd cycles) skinned as an archaeology dig grid; balance is exact equal team size (|c0-c1|<=1); the calibrated baseline is a deterministic hash-scrambled balanced partition (not the trivial all-same or geometric split), keeping F/B scale-invariant across grid sizes.
title: Trowel & Tessera: Splitting the Dig Grid
strategies: reference baseline: reproduce the deterministic hash-scrambled balanced partition (scores ~0.1)
one-pass swap greedy: from the reference, apply first-improvement crew swaps (i on crew 0 with j on crew 1) in a single sweep
best-improvement local search: from a fresh init, repeatedly commit the single most improving balance-preserving swap until convergence
multi-restart local search: deterministic perturbation restarts, keep the balanced assignment with the largest corroborated cut
